body.title: Add approach-frame as top-level palace rule (above persona)
body.result: Palace node theigors/rules/approach-frame written. States the positive-target principle, cites Elliot+Janoff-Bulman, carries revision: 2026-04-21 line. Auto-echoed to lab/theigors/. Commit 59e76863.
body.description:
Rules across the system are a mix of 'do X' (approach) and 'don't do Y' (proscriptive). Decision 2026-04-21: canonicalize approach-frame as the first-class rule-shaping principle. Reference: approach goals (Elliot & Church 1997), prescriptive vs proscriptive norms (Janoff-Bulman). Akien's framing: 'maybe you need a thing to focus on instead of a thing to NOT focus on.'

**Affected files:** palace node `theigors/rules/approach-frame` (new, sits above persona in read-order). Table: clan.memory_palace (existing). No code files.

**Design rules:** no-sqlite: no persistence beyond existing clan.memory_palace; no-new-memory-schemas: new path only, not new table; test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: add the palace rule node. State the principle, cite Elliot+Janoff-Bulman, note that 'do-not' rule is the structural mismatch and will be dissolved by T-audit-cc-rules-approach-frame. Include revision: 2026-04-21 affordance.
- Out: rewriting existing rules (T-audit-cc-rules-approach-frame). Out: versioning infrastructure (T-palace-rules-versioned).

**Test plan:** no new tests. Verification: rule readable at theigors/rules/approach-frame via `psql ... SELECT content FROM memory_palace WHERE path = 'theigors/rules/approach-frame'`. /review Mode A Check 7 picks it up automatically next session.
